rows.snippet: Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) 2 years ago TLS certificate is required before using the charts component." or "Failed to connect to Laserfiche Audit Trail Charts Component." These errors can occur if you bypass the... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014189/laserfiche-audit-trail-11-frequen ...
rows.title: Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
rows.detailTitle: Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) Product: Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 KB: 1014189 Published: March 25, 2021 Last Updated: July 11, 2023 Q: The default columns for each event type look different in this new version of Audit Trail. Where can I find the columns from the previous version of Audit Trail? To make Audit Trail reports more consistent, some of the columns have been reorganized into groups. Previously, all columns were displayed as a flat list. Now they are arranged into multiple tiers. For example, all user related properties are now organized as sub-columns under User and all entry related properties are located under Resource as sub-columns. You can directly click the compound columns to view the sub-column details. You can still select any of those sub-columns to view as top columns in the report by using the Configure Column Display dialog box. Q: Are there additional filters other than the filters in the left pane? Click the funnel icons on each column header to add advanced filters. The filter criteria will be displayed above the report grid. Q: Can I see additional details on changes listed in the Changes column? Click on the change listed in the Changes column to view a dialog box of changes related to the audit event. The first line is the object name or ID affected by the changes, for example, a metadata field name and its field ID. The contents below the first line are divided into groups, each of which contains the name of the changed property, the value before change, and the value after change. For example, the property name could be Field Type, the before value could be number, and the after value could be string. Q: Can I re-index the search catalog manually? Yes, you can re-index a search catalog by removing and re-adding the date ranges. For previous installations (before Update 4), perform the following steps to re-index the search catalog: Locate the search catalog location by viewing the Search Engine tab in the Audit Trail Configuration page. Detach the search catalog by clicking the Detach button. Delete the catalog folder from step 1. Click the Attach button to select the same folder name (or specify another path) on the Search Engine configuration page to create a new catalog. Q: Can Audit Trail 11 reuse reporting data from an Audit Trail 10 SQL database? No. Audit Trail 11 will only migrate the data from archived audit logs. It will not migrate reporting data from an Audit Trail 10 reporting SQL database. However, it will migrate saved report definitions. Q: What should I do if an audit event I'm expecting is not included in a report? If the audit events are not managed by the Laserfiche Server, ensure the respective audit log files are in folders listed in the Repositories tab on the Audit Trail Configuration page. Ensure the audit events are within the date ranges in the Search Catalogs tab on the Audit Trail Configuration page. Re-index audit catalogs for all date ranges that include the audit events. For previous installations (before Update 4): If the audit events are not managed by the Laserfiche Server, ensure the respective audit log files are in folders listed in the Repositories tab on the Audit Trail Configuration page. Ensure the audit events are within the data age in the Search Engine tab. Re-index audit catalogs of the repository. See above for how to re-index. Q: Previously, I could directly access the audit reporting SQL database to run my own queries and generate my own audit reports or charts as an automatic scheduled task. Can I directly read the search catalog? Audit Trail 11 uses the Laserfiche Full Text Search Engine (LFFTS) to index the audit data in order to provide a better report generation experience. As the search catalog schema is undocumented and subject to change, Audit Trail 11 includes a small toolkit (PowerShell script) to export reports as Microsoft Excel files. Use Windows scheduled tasks to create an automated task that runs the script. By default, the Excel file is a TAB-delimited CSV format file. By default, the script is located in the Samples subfolder of your Audit Trail installation folder: C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Audit Trail\Samples\Export-AuditReport.ps1 The basic workflow should be: On the Audit Trail Reporting page, design a report by applying filters as desired. Use the Configure Column Display dialog box to select the columns to include in the report. Save the report and note the report ID visible from the browser address bar: https://ServerName/AuditTrail/reports/ABCD1234EFGH-IJKLM?lang=en&dataSource=repositoryandserver&view=table Open the Task Scheduler on Windows to create a scheduled task Use this task to run the PowerShell script with the following parameters Export-AuditReport.ps1 -Repository "repositoryname(servername)" -ReportId "ABCD1234EFGH-IJKLM" -ExportFile "reportfilename.xlsx" By default, the exported report file will be located in C:\ProgramData\Las

rows.title: List of Changes for the June 2020 Laserfiche App
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for the June 2020 Laserfiche App
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for the June 2020 Laserfiche App Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1014176 Published: July 1, 2020 Last Updated: February 22, 2023 Summary The Laserfiche app is an Android, iOS, and Windows app that lets you capture, upload, and securely access and work with documents and forms in and outside your Laserfiche repository while on the go. This article highlights new features and changes for the Laserfiche Mobile Server, Android app, iOS app, and Windows app. Overview of New Features Android, iOS, and Windows Apps This app supports the Laserfiche Forms 10.4.4 new functionality that hides action history from the form submitter. (246516) You can now recover form data when the form fails to submit and task data when a task fails to submit in Laserfiche Forms. (236465) Additional Changes When Always use Windows authentication is selected on the LFDSSTS page, the sign-in screen now loads successfully on iOS. (239242) The hyperlink to open a form from an email now opens the form successfully. (244797) The Barcode icon in Forms is now displayed properly. (206128) App Versions iOS: 2020.6.29 Android: 2020.6.19 Windows: 2020.6.26 Related Links 1014099 Release Notes for the Laserfiche 10.4.2 App 1013813 The Difference Between Electronic and Digital Signatures in the Laserfiche App 1012782 The Difference Between the web client and Laserfiche Mobile Links Laserfiche Mobile Administration help files Related Links 1014230 Laserfiche App Releases 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: An "Application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005)." Error Occurs When Starting the Laserfiche Client Through a Citrix Session.
rows.detailTitle: An "Application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005)." Error Occurs When Starting the Laserfiche Client Through a Citrix Session.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005)." Error Occurs When Starting the Laserfiche Client Through a Citrix Session. Product: Laserfiche 7 KB: 1011905 Published: December 3, 2007 Last Updated: December 3, 2007 Summary When you attempt to start the Laserfiche Client through a Citrix session, you may receive the following error message: Application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005). Click on OK to terminate. Cause Citrix Hotfix PSE450R01W2K3035 may not be compatible with the Laserfiche Client. Resolution Please see the following Citrix Knowledge Base article for a registry workaround. You can specify that the Laserfiche Client process should be excluded from being monitored by the Citrix Multiple Monitor Hook feature. http://support.citrix.com/article/ctx110301 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Could not load file or assembly 'LFLicenseReader.DLL' or one of its dependencies. The specified module could not be found." Error Occurs When Loading The Laserfiche Forms Website. Product: Laserfiche Forms 9 KB: 1013250 Published: May 13, 2013 Last Updated: May 13, 2013 Summary When loading Laserfiche Forms, you may receive the following error message: Could not load file or assembly ''LFLicenseReader.DLL'' or one of its dependencies. The specified module could not be found. Cause This issue can occur when the computer hosting Laserfiche Forms does not have the 32-bit version of Visual C++ Redistributable for Visual Studio 2012 Update 1 installed. Resolution Download and install the 32-bit installation of Visual C++ Redistributable for Visual Studio 2012 Update 1 from Microsoft.com: http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/confirmation.aspx?id=30679 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 6 3 years ago https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014397/list-of-changes-for-import-agent-10-4-0-update-6 Import Agent Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Import ... ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014397/list-of-changes-for-import-age ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 6
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 6
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 6 Product: Import Agent KB: 1014397 Published: October 11, 2022 Last Updated: December 13, 2022 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 6. This update has been superseded by KB 1014408 Import Agent 10.4.0 Update 7. Fixes for Import Agent Import Agent will no longer freeze when the OCR engine cannot process a file. Instead, when the file times out, Import Agent will continue with other files. (365977) An issue with the Zimbra mail server that was causing emails to get stuck in the Import Agent working folder has been fixed. (371212) You can now customize the name of the Laserfiche Email Archive Service Working Folder. (389996) Email archive now tries to import all emails at each polling instead of limiting itself to 10 emails per polling. (393362) The %(EmailRecipients) token now returns a value when importing messages. (397029) Known Issue for Import Agent Import Agent service and Email Archive service requires to restart service after install patch. (398485) Files The update includes the following files for Import Agent: ImportAgentService.exe (10.4.0.202) The update includes the following files for Email Archive: Laserfiche.EmailArchive.exe (10.4.0.140) Laserfiche.EmailArchive.Logging.dll (10.4.0.140) Laserfiche.EmailArchive.S22.Imap.dll (10.4.0.140) ProfileSchema.xsd Resolution To update Import Agent: Click the following link to download the update: KB1014397.zip On the computer hosting Import Agent, extract and run LFImportAgent_1040202_KB1014397_Update6.exe. Start the Import Agent and Email Archive Services from (Control Panel -> Administrative Tools -> Services), or restart the computer the update was installed on. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Import Agent 7 Cannot Import Laserfiche Briefcases Exported from Laserfiche Client 7.2.x. Product: Import Agent 7 KB: 1011681 Published: October 30, 2006 Last Updated: October 30, 2006 Summary Import Agent 7 cannot import Laserfiche briefcases that were created from Laserfiche Client 7.2.x. The Windows Event Viewer will contain a "Decompression of briefcase failed" error message for the Import Agent service. Cause Laserfiche 7.2 uses a different format to create briefcases. Briefcases exported from Laserfiche client 7.2 are in tar.gz format. Import Agent expects a briefcase to be in .zip format and is unable to import Laserfiche 7.2 briefcases. Note: You can still export briefcases from a Laserfiche 7.2 repository using Laserfiche client 7.0.x or 7.1.x. The exported briefcase will be in zip format and can be imported as normal by Import Agent 7. Related Links Please see the following Knowledge Base article for additional information. 1011000 A "Failed to Import New Document - Decompression Failed [7434]" Error Occurs When Importing a Briefcase. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
